name: Fresh Double Berry Pie
recipeIngredient: 5 cups assorted fresh or frozen raspberries and blackberries (can use canned, drain well)
1 package (15-oz) PILLLSBURY All Purpose Pie Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine 1-1/4 cups sugar, cornstarch, and tapioca; stir until well mixed.
2. Add berries and liqueur to the mixture; toss gently.
3. Prepare pie crust according to package directions for a filled one-crust pie in a 9-inch pie plate.
4. Flute the edges and bake at 450°F (232°C) for 9 to 11 minutes. If the crust puffs up, gently press it back into the bottom and sides of the dish with the back of a wooden spoon.
5. Unfold the remaining pie crust sheet; sprinkle with flour.
6. Using a 3-inch heart-shaped cookie cutter, cut out enough hearts to overlap around the edge of the pie. Reserve extra pie crust.
7. Spoon the filling into the prepared pastry shell. Arrange the hearts, overlapping slightly, with tips pointing toward the center.
8. Using a heart-shaped canape cutter, cut the remaining pie crust sheet into heart shapes. Place small hearts over the filling (turn in the opposite direction from the larger hearts).
9. Place the pie on a baking sheet and bake at 375°F (190°C) for 1 hour.
10. Sprinkle the remaining tablespoon of sugar and cinnamon over the pies.
11. Cool completely before serving. Yield: one 9-inch pie. Kitchen Ease: Cover the edge of the pie crust with strips of foil after 15 to 20 minutes of baking to prevent excessive browning.
